channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT — 94 replies — 376.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-07-04 11:04 UTC

OP

>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Early replies (first 3)

62433628
But saaaaaar isnt this normal in india and all civilized places? Only in the so called united states of america they kick their kids out

62433637
>>62433628
anything that isnt jewish is indian because.... because it just is, okay!!!!

62433641
All you need is a half million dollar down payment and you can get your mortgage payment down to $4,250 per month on your loan against a million dollar house. Then you can live in the desert on top of your neighbors with trees that will fall over during the first rains and slight breeze because they don't belong in a desert.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442419
>>62442346
Let's take it easy and look first into the mirror before judging others. Not everyone is fit to be a parent. Hopefully your child inherits a good stepfather, which would be a better outcome than inheriting your will.

62442422
>>62442391
what i am trying to articulate in my post might require an IQ of 110 to maybe process. because its slightly nuanced. but its really not that complicated, fundamentally.

my dad wanted a WASP-y son who had fratbro hobbies and a kind of upper class lifestyle. golf, wedding photos, friend group of likeminded frat bros who go to sports games and go out drinking together. but my dad was convinced by talking heads like buffet and ramsey that any financial support for your kids after their early 20s is wrong. so he took that advice and cut off all support. and i, having no support in college, basically had to learn totally by myself how to function and rely on just myself - to the point of becoming self reliant, at the cost of having no appetite for frivolous spending, luxury, or expensive/high maintenance relationships once i entered the workforce. understand- i am not complaining whatsoever, i am just saying, if you want a preppy rich kid socialite son with a hot high maintenance girlfriend, youre probably gonna need to get him a job. if you dont want to because that will spoil him or whatever, thats perfectly fine, just dont be surprised when he finds his own financial advice (eg sam hyde lol), like you found yours, and he decides to go full hermitmaxx mode and he daytrades with his paychecks instead of buying golf clubs or a saving up for an annual trip to europe for his high maintenance hot gf

does this make any sense? am i crazy here?

62442430
>>62442422

son you need to go MAKE SOMETHING of yourself

62442437
>>62442422
Your father is wrong to dictate the life he wants you to live. You are meant to bond with your parents if you both have a good relationship, sometimes helping each other if possible and within reason. Never burden a child like that.

62442543
>>62438432
don't you see the "it is in a different currency"?
it's obviously not in euros u dumbass. European countries have a few more currencies.

62442989
>>62442194
>>62442389
>>62442422
cringe and victimcomplex

62443265
>>62442989
Dads who want to control the lives of their kids without supporting them are just delusional retards as evidenced by the lashing out in response to those posts.

If my boss wants me to do work for him, he pays me. If a business wants me to try their product, they give me incentives for a trial period. If daddy wants his son to live a certain way it's going to take more than a regal command. You wanted to teach your kid self reliance by kicking him out, right? Well now your kid doesn't rely on you and has zero obligation to follow your desired path. Mission accomplished, you successfully removed yourself from having influence.

62443320
>>62442989
>changing his ID
>still trying to lash out at random posters

Yeah the only sad lonely faggot here is you

62443525
26% of US men 25-29 live at home. If you are single and have a good relationship with your family there really is no reason not to do it, you can get some decent money together

62443887
>>62442422
Why not just pay the high quality lifestyle with your own money?

62444170
>>62442422
Buffett gave his kids $90k (roughly $600-700k in today's money) in Berkshire stock when they turned 19.
Peter started a record business, Howard started an excavator business, and I can't remember what Susie did (something involving art galleries), but they all squandered it, with barely a second thought, which is shocking given how much Buffett harps on
>buy and hold
Clearly, he didn't instill that virtue in his own children.
He helped them somewhat in others ways: boguht a farm for Howard, but charged him rent.
He also notoriously would bribe them to not be fat, subjecting them to weighings, as well as offering them rewards if he failed to maintain a given weight. (Not sure if this is based or psychotic.)

62444184
>>62444170
>He helped them somewhat in others ways: boguht a farm for Howard, but charged him rent.
>He also notoriously would bribe them to not be fat, subjecting them to weighings, as well as offering them rewards if he failed to maintain a given weight. (Not sure if this is based or psychotic.)
in the case of the farm, btw, the rent was based in part on how much Howard weighed.
My point was that Buffett actually did give a great deal of assistance to his kids, even if he was a skinflint in other respects.
And his kids mostly squandered it.

62444191
>>62444170
>Buffett gave his kids $90k (roughly $600-700k in today's money) in Berkshire stock when they turned 19.
This was in addition to paying for their college (which none of them finished), btw.

62444231
>>62443887
why would i pay for golf clubs and polos and fancy watches and a WASP-y country club lifestyle when thats not what i personally want?

after a decade of being on my own, i just want to retire as soon as possible and spend as little as possible. even if it means being a single hermit, i dont really care. i was on my own, so i had to determine what was best for me. now, at the eleventh hour, i am being accosted for "being weird" because what i want and what work towards isnt what my dad expected i would do, basically. again, im not complaining. im just raising the questionn, how does one delicately explain that when you give zero suport, you take yourself away from influencing outcomes.

62444276
>>62442989
to be totally honest i see my father as the one was wronged, by the bad parenting advice that comes from financial talking heads like ramsey and buffet who insist to boomers they need to cut off support to their kids at 18. my dad could have had a son with the lifestyle he wanted to see his son have. he just thought it would magically happen with no financial or job finding help. i actually feel very sorrowful with respect to the situation, i think it was a perfectly avoidable, unoptimal outcome for everyone involved.
i dont see myself as a victim at all, i have a very fortunate life in many ways.

62444331
>>62443265
i dont think my dad ever wanted to necessarily give regal commands from afar, he just assumed- i believe- that despite having a son who was an indebted college kid totally on their own, he thought i was going to gravitate towards the same life path he followed and all his friends kids followed. but after being thrown in the deep end of life, i decided i wanted to retire ASAP even if it means living like an impoverished NEET for 10 years, rather than retire at 65 after decades of living with an extravagent country club WASP-y lifestyle. i dont think the possibility entered his mind that someone could consider "success" in life as something different than boater shoes and pickleball and golfing at the country club. and to be clear, i would have been happy to be a Brayden with a 2.1 GPA in business and a job set up for me. but im also happy being a pseudo NEET goblin emulating Phantasy Star Online for gamecube and daytrading my salary this week, instead of spending a few hundred dollars golfing. honestly its whatever.

62444339
This thread only only shows how demoralization won on both sides of the coin. There is an outside enemy that want you destroyed and nothing less, not that you don’t deserve it with the way things have regressed.

62444345
>>62444276
>and buffet
See >>62444170
>>62444184
>>62444191
Buffett did not "cut off" his kids at 18. He gave them each $90k ($500-600k adjusted for inflation) in BRK stock on their 19th birthday, in addition to paying their college, and other stuff (but the "other stuff" usually had strings attached).
Howard Buffett's BRK b-day gift would be worth $236,262,411 today if he never sold any shares.

62444418
>>62433626
Portagees build an illegal second house in their backyard for their kids

62444615
>>62442194
Yeah I don’t think I’m going to get married or have kids either. I’m 30 and I see no point. I’m grinding hard and why would I make it even harder for myself?


>>62442338
This thread is weird, this is jewish and Epstein coded. Malicious bot activity. I’m finally catching on to the demoralizing bullshit. You people are evil.

62444686
>>62442380
>Winston Cruze
This guy gives off major "jock bully" vibes.
I hate him just off appearances alone.
He looks like he's had everything handed to him on a silver platter his entire life.

62444698
>>62444345
Yes. /I/ am aware that Buffet and Ramsey did not cut their kids off at 18. /I/ am aware that Buffet and Ramsey both gave their kids golden parachutes and millions of dollars in opportunities and stocks. /I/ am aware of all of this information. However, my father- like many boomers- took the advice of financial talking heads like Ramsey and Buffet and /listened to what they said/ rather than /observe how those men actually behaved/.

Does this make sense? Do you understand how I am able to parse information where my father was not able to? I recognize many boomer parents simply /trusted what Ramsey and Buffet had to say/ instead of /observing what these men did with respect to their own children/.

All boomer financial talking heads are the same. They are all about "Do as I say, not as I do". For example, Benjamin Graham writes a book 'Intelligent Investor' telling normies to approach their finances with a Boglehead-tier attitude, only investing in VOO and other boomer stocks. Meanwhile Graham himself in the same book explains that he only became mega-rich because he YOLO'd into Geico.

62444823
>>62441222
>the value of a dollar
What value? It's worthless paper, this isn't 1971 anymore.

62444840
>>62444823

Whippersnappers still don’t get it

62444919
>>62444615
>this is jewish and Epstein coded. Malicious bot activity. I’m finally catching on to the demoralizing bullshit. You people are evil.

62444923
>>62444698
You seem untrustworthy and probably stole from your parents

62444924
>>62444823
You are dumb and repeat incel doom porn

62444981
>>62444615
Personally I actually would have loved to have started a family by now, but when you're thrown into the deep end of life and explicitly told- in no uncertain terms- no life raft will be provided to you... At least for me, that totally evaporated any and all romantic or family-starting instincts within me, for a long time. My only priority for years was finding stability and securing solid income and investments. After all, I personally watched multiple friends in college flunk out of our degree program after becoming depressed after a bad break up. Why would I risk dating when a bad breakup could end up in me throwing away my GPA, and internship/job prospects, as a potential result?

Now that I have stable income and a solid portfolio, now that I have breathing room, it's uncomfortably apparent how unnecessary the whole ordeal was and how much happier everyone in the situation would be, if boomers weren't convinced en masse that an adult kid asking for money in college was, in every case, a parasitic leech.

62445011
>>62444981
Waaaaaaah the boomers didn't give me a handout only a hand up

62445030
>>62445011
I am content with the outcome. You are arguing with a hallucination.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 101 replies — 346.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 101 • Updated: 2026-07-04 10:56 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62438410
>>62438328
No.

62439167
>>62433595
Saw the Aegis partnership, now Utila too. They're lining up some solid collaborations.

62439181
>>62437631
loved the confidence

62439236
>>62438282
then why are you here

62439758
>>62438074
With that risk model, I don't think anyone needs to hand over their custody.

Self custody is part of what it's designed for.

62440739
>>62439758
what about in an exchange like binance

62441904
>>62439167
Maybe they have more coming that we just don't know about yet. It's actually a good sign that they're still building despite the current market conditions.

62442213
>>62440739
Kraken

62442214
>>62426512
Buy the dip, obviously.

62442227
>>62439167
But that won't help the price up

62442228
>>62442214
Now???

62442255
>>62426512
I dca 60% of my savings each month while saving the remaining 40% for potential lower lows as we approach October, at which point I'll start going in with all of my monthly savings and that 40% I've been holding back.

62442503
>>62442227
Maybe not immediately. But without infrastructure, there's nothing to drive long-term value anyway.

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228
I dollar cost. THe past few months have been gr8

62442553
>>62442511
Where do you do that

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

item.title: Trump fucked you — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Trump fucked you

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-07-04 11:08 UTC

OP

>His annual disclosure for 2025 with the US Office of Government Ethics, disclosed that Trump made $636 million in royalties from CIC Digital, LLC, a company that issues $TRUMP, a crypto token that he launched three days before taking office for his second term. Almost all of the come came in the form of royalties from a license agreement with Celebration Coins. Trump's meme coin reached its peak value of $74.24 within a day of launching. On Tuesday evening, its price was $1.67, down 98% since inception.

>The president also reported almost $600 million in income from token sales by World Liberty Financial, a cryptocurrency company backed by the president and his family, and around $65 million from equity sales in the firm that controls World Liberty Financial. Another $196 million in equity sales of Stablecoin Holdco LLC are also listed. A crypto firm and issuer of tokens, World Liberty Financial was launched during the 2024 presidential campaign. It was co-founded by the president, his sons Eric and Donald Jr. and Zack and Alex Witkoff, the sons of U.S. special envoy and longtime Trump friend Steve Witkoff.

Eternal aristocratic levels of wealth were transferred from plebs directly to Trump's family via shitcoin scams.

Early replies (first 3)

62433591
>>62433580
>Zack and Alex Witkoff, the sons of U.S. special envoy and longtime Trump friend Steve Witkoff.
I recently learned what background this family has. I was naive to think Witkoff wasnt.....
>>>62433555

62433599
>>62433580
>>62433591
Imagine his sons short when he is cooperating with the cz cartel 1 minute before his retarded tweets and cex dumps! Its just the top of the (((berg)))

62433604
>>62433599
But you know something something in the casino.....

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433618
>>62433580
LMAO CRYPTO KEKS FUCKED IN THE ASS ONCE AGAIN
HOW MANY UNLUBED COCKS HAVE YOU TAKEN IN YOUR ASS NOW?
FOUR... FIVE HUNDRED? MAYBE SIX HUNDRED?
GET RAPED IN YOUR SAGGY BUTTHOLE CRYPTO KEKS

62433624
>>62433618
If you missed out in the past you can still buy in around 55k(u wont)

62433629
>>62433618
Only the most retarded of retards bought and holded TRUMP coin after the first day.

62433632
>>62433629
on any day.

62433672
>>62433632
I STILL mad i did not buy at the first hours, i saw some /biz/ thread but it wasnt clear that it was real and i was tried. Would have been at fucking 5 Dollar. I of course would have aggressively sold the way up and dumped all at lower then 50. Would have made so much money. So no, people buying at the first day to dump high were not dumb.

62433743
>>62433672
You would have bought at $7 and sold at $2.58.
Trumpenberg would be thankful for your contribution (he wouldn't be).

62433763
>>62433580
So the crypto president and his retarded sons finally killed crypto for good. They should probably shutdown WLFI and their mining and treasury companies at this point. Nobody will ever invest with this scumbags again

62433764
>>62433599
I look forward to the years-long imbroglio of the next administration investigating all of their financial crimes

62433798
>>62433763
Ohboi, are you new to this planet? The 30iq hillbilly niggers ll never learn,

62433862
He had 6b when he entered office, these numbers are nothing, would have made more holding spy

62433864
>>62433580
Nobody cares, amerigolems will defend this no matter how bad the corruption and decay gets because they get to pretend they're winning the (((culture war))) that the yids manufactured to win no matter what.

62433948
>>62433864
Name 1 eu politician less corrupt than trump

62433962
>>62433580
Yeah it’s disgusting I definitely regret voting for him in some ways. And even though I didn’t actually buy any of his shitcoins, I still paid for it because it sucked all the liquidity up and then he dumped on everyone, cratering the entire market and single handedly wrecking the bull run. Then he wrecked it again with tariffs. Then he wrecked it again with the Iran war. I don’t think I’ll be voting again. Fuck the government.

62433974
>>62433618
After you hit the wall, unlubed cock inside you doesn’t even make you cry out loud.

62435237
>>62433618
>>62433974
these are strangely visceral and unnecessarily erotic posts. are you anons speaking from experience????? never bought these coins btw, just don't get why anons are speaking like this

62435296
>>62433580
I voted for this

62436883
you lost tranny

62436896
/biz/ fucked me not trump
I've had several winning plays the last 18 months and each time I was fudded out by retards on this board
It made me realize I can't listen to you morons because you have no clue what is going on.

62436905
>>62433672
It was fucking listed on solana, I don't use solana. Sucks.

62436906
>>62433580
yeah but it has nothing to do with the market performance of crypto. trump family crypto holdings are taking open market losses just like every other bagholder here. this is the classic trump play of royalty & licensing.

62437518
>>62433580
How are his cultists spinning this?

62437570
>>62436896
Most of the posters here are penniless 3rd world browns giving bad advice on purpose because they think it's funny.

62440880
>>62433580
The crypto president lol

62441152
>>62433580
When is he and his crooked family getting charged for this?

62443718
>>62433580
>>62433618
Now the news are he also bought stocks the day before he eased tariffs. Its just comial self enrichment
>>62433743
NO I WOULDNT
I would have sold ALL the way up, in fact im always selling early and after x3 i for sure would have takenout initials and at 30 i would have sold most. Damn.
>>62436905
Same but it was instantly listed on kucoin and i think i had 2k there.....

62445022
>>62433580
A literal crypto scammer, is the idol the the patriots worldwide, and the president of the people of america.

62445029
Imagine being the guy who loses his life savings to Trump, then gets shipped off to die for Israel.

62445036
>>62445029
That is a good goy for you.

item.title: POLYMARKET CONFIRMED TO BE KIKED TO THE MAX — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
POLYMARKET CONFIRMED TO BE KIKED TO THE MAX

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-07-04 11:07 UTC

OP

a literal fucking mossad netanyahu laundering operation LOL!!!!! no wonder they always refuse to pay out when you win and everythings rigged.

>>>/pol/538318173

Early replies (first 3)

62444716
>>62444709
Nobody cares about crypto or gambling, fuck off with this /pol/ shit

62444718
>>62444716

62444725
>>62444709
Cattle who gamble in a casino they don’t own deserve to be taken advantage of

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444731
>>62444716
Kys r*ddit kike

62444750
Months ago, I told you retards that tokenization and gambling will replace the employment market but no one believed me. I saw a video today about people playing sport in basements in eastern europe so that other people can bet on them winning. Everything is going to be tokenized, and betted on. This is the new economy for the masses that are about to become jobless.

62444752
The other interesting thing is what thiel is doing with replacing politics with companies in south america. The world is actively changing under you but no one is paying attention because they are too busy arguing about whether ai will replace jobs when the billionaires have already price it in.

62444757
>>62444709
Imagine giving liquidity to jews!

62444779
>>62444716

62444793
>>62444709
>call them schizo

62444804
>>62444716
>Crawls out of the woodwork within fractions of a second

62444809
>>62444716
>no one cares about finance on finance board
shalom

62444817
>>62444793
lol i worked for this group for a cpl weeks

62444819
>>62444750
I'd rather erect some guillotines. Hubris like this will be their downfall.

62444833
>>62444716
>There’s a Hebrew in this thread RIGHT NOW

62444835
>>62444817
>work
Do they pay you in memecoin or feet pics?

62444863
>>62444716
Oy vey shut it down

62444874
Kind of reminds me of people who took the vaccines, when it came out that it contains human embryo content the vaxies just memory holed it like they aren't cannibal gmo mutants

Epstein token bitcoin also gets memory holed a lot

62444876
>>62444874
Why would embryo content even matter? Please answer without emotion.

62444882
>>62444876
Lol ugly circumcised cannibal mutant

62444901
>>62444819
Your actions are not going to have any effect anon. They have the ability to deploy humanoids to quell dissent. What i see playing out is they'll have command of the cities while the people who don't like it will be left to roam around. If you think some napoleon style revolution is coming then you are hopelessly delusional. The next revolution is not going to be violent because they now have the monopoly on it.

62444907
>>62444718
>>62444731
>>62444779
>>62444804
>>62444833

Each and every single one of you is gonna receive a 3 day ban for racism.

Enjoy it.

62444913
>>62444901
If the money is worthless no one will feel like doing their job, there will probably be revolutions but it'll be chaos, probably everything will burn, it'll be nigger rioting and stealing but on a huge scale, because no more imports, no production capacity

62444917
>>62444809
Polymarket is finance the same way Avatar (blue people movie) is anime.

62444954
>>62444913
The chaos, It will not last. Like I said, they will control the cities which will be the most stable locations and people will capitulate if they want to enjoy that stability. Revolution is not pretty, it doesn't feed you or make you safe. We are basically going back to antiquity. The only places that will not change is probably only china because the government has total control, but everywhere else will be a lab for them to experiment how the world should function in a post ai world.

62444979
>>62444709
it's gambling

62444984
>>62444709
No shit.

62444992
>>62444954
>The large metro areas will be safest!
>Packed full of hungry, jobless people with nothing left to lose!

62444993
>>62444954
Yeah it won't last because everything will be burned down quickly because you lot live in cardboard houses. Who is this 'they' that you're wo subservient to, I can assure you that whoever you think 'they' are will have looted you and be long gone when tshtf

62445007
>>62444992
>>62444993
Full of compliant jobless people. The cities have all the power, internet and water infrastructure so they are obviously the most valuable real estate. Your idea of a revolution will not play out. It's funny in january i was arguing with a scitard about how data centers would be built in places they shouldn't but he kept trying to come up with counter examples like them using ocean instead and I told him they would always take a short cut, now look at what's happening today. You are using an outdated model of the world to reason about this. You simply don't understand how fast everything is moving? These people can put up automated turrets at every building and monitor traffic in real time if they wanted to. Stop believing hollywood slop about people rising up. It won't work. They have tools that can do surveillance at a scale that you can't imagine. They just need a crisis to deploy them at full power. Whatever story or film you watched that promised you revolution is outdated and obsolete. You need to update your thinking because things are moving too fast now.

62445032
>>62444709
The neo department of truth turned out to be a kike construct?! WHOA

62445034
>>62444716
Spotted one

item.title: Post country and how much PTO you get >aus >5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick — 27 replies — 108.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Post country and how much PTO you get >aus >5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-07-04 11:12 UTC

OP

Post country and how much PTO you get
>aus
>5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick

Early replies (first 3)

62443952
US
"Flexible" but I took seven weeks last year

62443958
>>62443952

If you’re white collar professional is the USA really a nightmare for employees like everyone says ? Or it’s over exaggerated?

62443972
>>62443958
Depends on the company I guess. I've always imagined that your average retard that complains about a lack of PTO works fast food, retail, or something equally menial

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62443986
>mex
>~20 days + holidays

62444027
>burger
>3.5 weeks

62444080
>>62443950
>US
>32 days
All PTO is lumped into one pool: sick days, holidays, vacation, same shit.

62444094
>>62444080

That’s great! I’d much rather have combined PTO than the Aus system where sick leave is seperate, since I never use it all. What do you do for work?

62444108
>>62444094
Structural engineer. Only people who have been with the company for ten years+ get that much PTO.

62444126
>>62443950
Us government

>~2 weeks sick paid
>~2 weeks annual leave paid
>Every federal holiday off
>3 months paid paternity leave (expires a year after birth)
>1 year "family leave" unpaid

62444133
America

I don't go to work I just sell shit online and gamble on lines

62444135
>>62443950
12 sick, 15 PTO, then we get 9 federal holidays, Christmas eve and my birthday off. Then on a de facto basis via declarations we normally get black friday and half days on the day before Thanksgiving and Christmas eve. So 38-40 days off a year.

>US state public employee

62444144
>>62443950
US
28 days ATO (PTO and sick leave)
it doesn't matter though, i'm paid strictly on productivity.

62444155
>>62443950
Canada
3 weeks PTO :(
11 days sick
2 family leave days

62444271
>>62443950
NZ
>2 weeks PTO.
>2 weeks Sick.
>4 weeks domestic abuse leave.
Bit awkward ringing the boss and telling him I can't come in because I'm beating his daughter, but such is life.

62444329
>>62443950
USA.
Unlimited PTO
Unlimited sick time
You'll get fired for being cheeky with it, but having a woman dedicated to tracking your vacation/holiday time and whether or not your sick is extremely low-trust society tier behavior. Spiritually canadian.

62444336
>>62443950
I get 2 weeks in canada
last year I forgot to use them

62444340
France
7 weeks paid leave

62444341
>>62443950
>US
>2.5 weeks vacation
>1 week "personal business"
>4 weeks sick

62444369
>>62443950
USA
>2 weeks sick
>4 weeks vac

62444406
>>62444155

Canada seems like a worse version of Australia no offence lol

62444407
>>62444271
Nz is 4 weeks annual leave right?

62444410
>>62444329

Don’t companies give unlimited PTO because the research has shown it causes employees to take less time off, not more? Since most want to Err on the side of caution.

Also how does it work if you leave the company? Here we get paid out our leave (not sick tho) in cash when we quit.

62444855
>>62443950
USA
25 days

29 next year.

62444899
>>62443950
USA 52 weeks. Get rich and own your time bitches

62444988
>>62444094
>since I never use it all
Why not? Don't sleep great one night and just take a sick day the next. Not abusing sick days is cucked

62445023
ItalyAfrica.
3 weeks

62445041
>>62444988

Yea I’m planning to, will just keep 5 days spare just in case

item.title: >YOU'RE A MILLION DOLLARS IN DEBT? NOOOOOOOOO YOU CAN'T DECLARE BANKRUPTCY LIKE I DID. YOU BETTER WORK 3 FULL TIME JOBS… — 16 replies — 53.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>YOU'RE A MILLION DOLLARS IN DEBT? NOOOOOOOOO YOU CAN'T DECLARE BANKRUPTCY LIKE I DID. YOU BETTER WORK 3 FULL TIME JOBS…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-07-04 10:55 UTC

OP

>YOU'RE A MILLION DOLLARS IN DEBT? NOOOOOOOOO YOU CAN'T DECLARE BANKRUPTCY LIKE I DID. YOU BETTER WORK 3 FULL TIME JOBS WITH SIDE HUSTLES AND SHIT RICE AND BEANS UNTIL EVERY LAST CENT IS PAID IN FULL

Why are boomers such hypocrites?

Early replies (first 3)

62441296
>>62441262

> I don’t want you to make the same mistakes I did, son

62442584
>>62441262
>I refuse to learn from your misteaks just let me fuck my life up in pieces

62442587
>>62441296
>>62442584
Why would anyone take financial advice from someone who went bankrupt

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442596
>>62442587
being able to put yourself back together after a calamity is a skill some people could learn from
ramsay is a grifter though

62442597
>>62442587
a) They can recognize the signs of somebody making their mistake, and give valid advice on how to avoid it/recover from it.
b) He who has never failed has never tried.
c) The perfect is the enemy of the good.
d) Your mother will die in her sleep tonight.

62442744
>>62442596
>>62442597
Fuck off Dave

62442749
>>62442587
Why would I take financial advice from someone who was always rich? They don't understand how hard it is for someone like me....

Can't win

62442805
>>62442597
Fuck you

62442881
>>62442597

62443344
>>62442749
>They don't understand how hard it is for someone like me....
This attitude is why you'll always be poor. You enjoy being poor, it gives you something to whine about, and in your feeble mind it can be used as an excuse for your continual failures. I used to be poor but my attitude was always "what can I do to become rich and how do I execute on that" not "oh woe is me I'm so poor why won't people feel sorry for me".

62443456
>>62443344
The point is you'd be a better person to learn financial planning from than someone who was born rich and just coasted.

62443568
>>62442749
If I wanted diet advice id rather get it from someone who used to be fat than someone who was always fit.

62444976
>>62441262
ducks and beans...

62445008
>>62441262
dave ramsey pressure washes my anus

62445013
>>62442587
Why would you let someone run your country who went bankrupt 7 or more times. So maybe shlomo owns his broke ass but still

62445017
>>62445013
TDS, he never went bankrupt

item.title: whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Eve… — 22 replies — 40.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Eve…

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-07-04 10:41 UTC

OP

whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Even if i had a few mill i would probably move there from USA.

Early replies (first 3)

62440816
Don't think you would, mate

62440835
>>62440727
Brilliant, best city in the world

62440843
>>62440727
lel london.

Moved away from Fulham and London 5 years ago thank fucking christ. Hell on earth.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62440865
>>62440843
Written by a jeet in Mumbai

62440873
>>62440727
its literal shit i genuinely dont understand what you find appealing about it ?

62440877
Check the demographic figures for London then come back here and tell us you still want to live there.

62440886
>>62440727
Not nice

62440968
>>62440727
Slave mind. But its good that you retards stay in the worst places in the world and leave thai beaches empty.

62440971
>>62440865
Mumbai and London is there a difference?

62440974
>>62440968
Thailand lmao how fucking depressing my god

62441031
>>62440974
>says the guy wanting to live in one of the worst cities in the world
>yeah pic related is depressing
>but concrete and a mix of jeets, niggers, moslems and assholes is good

62441040
A thread full of jeets seething because they don't live in London

62441046
>>62440727
>>62440816
>>62440835
>>62440865
>>62440873
Go back home. Nobody wants you to be in Europe.

62441083
>>62440727
Pretty sure it's like living in Pyongyang but browner

62441105
The trick is not to live in London but in the nice suburbs of the borders/surrounding home counties and enter London only for work and selected leisure

62441183
>>62441031
>he won’t ever respond to this

62444114
>>62441183
Yeah, it seems he left

62444152
Is this a troll post? What's so "unreal" about Chelsea?
Even if hustle and bustle is what you're after I'm pretty sure large American cities have more to offer given the higher purchasing power. 20% of Londoners are on unemployment benefits and then there's a large chunk of Londoners on full time employment who are too poor to afford anything and are also on benefits.
Add in all the illegal immigrants and non whites living under the radar and you get a city of 8 million people but the entertainment and night out options are there to cater for a city of 3 million or less because they're the only ones able to afford anything. When factor those things in London is about as vibrant as some mid-tier American city like Denver or Phoenix.

It's a bit like jeets bragging about the massive size of Mumbai not taking into account the fact Minneapolis has more people who are able to eat out once a week.

62444383
>>62440971
Mumbai has white people

62444388
>>62444152
I think I heard something like welfare obligations exceed tax receipts in the UK also, whole country is basically fucked

62444392
The UK is as poor as Mississippi, literally.

https://www.theatlantic.com/magazine/2026/07/uk-productivity-economy-reform-party/687303/

62445003
>>62440727
It's okay if you don't have to pay a mortgage or rent. The problem is it's overcrowded by the wrong people. Immigration from India is out of control and they are wrecking the place and turning it into a dump. Add to that there are Somalians everywhere and they contribute absolutely nothing. Their kids are animals. Then you've got the usual afro-black population growing (contribute nothing) but crime.

That said I've noticed an increasingly growing population of American elders here. They find the UK more peaceful and united than America apparently.

If you have a good job and earn above £45-50k, you can do ok, but not live expensively.

item.title: When you're part of the club, you literally can't fail — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
When you're part of the club, you literally can't fail

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-04 11:10 UTC

OP

I still remember the time when jeetlana was on its way to $ 0 and then all of a sudden vitalik resurrected it with just ONE tweet. Now it sits in the top five of L1 with no sign of falling off.

Early replies (first 3)

62444634
>>62444533
Its funny how it is so centralized, but looks like the "peephole" in it does not give a fuck, maybe i should start an excel sheet " chain" too.

62444635
Rent free is brutal out here, man. You know what it means—living rent free in someone’s head, no cost, no strings attached, but the mental toll? Oh, it’s real. Landchad’s out here dropping hints he’s in his 30s, fit as a fiddle, and willing to barter his way through life rather than pay a dime in cash. That’s next-level hustle—no money, just pure barter, survival of the fittest, no cap.

And the vibe? Landchad’s literally euphoric every single day, floating high on a cloud of confidence, happiness, and pure unfiltered bliss. Why ain’t anyone trying to be like that? I mean, it’s a no-brainer—living life on your own terms, no stress about rent, bills, or societal chains. Just existence, pleasure, and no cap, banging whore after whore every day if that’s your thing. No limits, no restrictions—just raw, unadulterated freedom.

It’s almost unfair how effortless he makes it seem. While most struggle to keep their heads above water, Landchad’s out here living like a king in his own realm, thriving in the chaos, embracing the chaos, and loving every second of it. This is the kind of lifestyle that makes you wonder—why settle for less when you can live rent-free, euphoric, and in total control? Seriously, man, the game’s changed, and Landchad’s out here showing us all how it’s done.

62444751
>>62444533
>vitalik resurrected it

how did vitalik do that? everything vitalik does is bearish, and solana is a competing chain, so im not sure what ure talking about

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444763
>>62444751
Vitalik tweeted once that solana is a so fucking cool chain or something(probably got paid for it or it was satire)
>700 validators

62444765
>>62444751
>https://x.com/VitalikButerin/status/1608591727316684804
He posted this when SOL was at $9. It marked the bottom and brought attention to Solana.

62444767
>>62444765
This does not surprise me, he is a cuck

62445039
>>62444635
kys brownoid with your ai posts

item.title: UK bros - HMRC and crypto — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UK bros - HMRC and crypto

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-04 11:11 UTC

OP

Do you have any experience with HMRC and crypto?

Anyone received nudge letters or notice to pay taxes on crypto??

All banking institutions are reporting money transfers to exchanges and HMRC are apparently coming after everyone.

Early replies (first 3)

62445035
Yes
If you ever interacted with a KYC exchange, they reported you to HMRC. I got the letter and paid up like a good goy.

62445038
>>62445035
Was it a nudge letter or a tax notice?

62445040
>>62445038
Nudge I guess. The CEX would have had my on chain transaction history so I didn't see the point in not paying and then later on being screwed for late tax payment + 7% interest on it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Post-work society — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Post-work society

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-07-04 11:12 UTC

OP

Am I correct in predicting that in the future only a few people will have a traditional job?

There's a guy who makes My Little Pony fleshlights out of silicone and sells them at 500 dollars a pop.
A lot of guys stream on Kick and make more than doctors.
Millions of young women shake their ass on Tiktok and Chaturbate, again making more than doctors.
The list goes on.
I'm starting to see a phenomenon where people no longer go to school, they all find some online niche to fill and then get rich.

Is this what everyone will be doing in 2050? Become an entertainer or die?
I've always hated the right-wing attitude that everyone has to make their own money. I want UBI, not this shit.

Early replies (first 3)

62445033
>the pedo elite won't give us UBI
>instead they give us platforms like Onlyfans where we humiliate ourselves for the right to continue existing

They're making fun of us.

62445042
We don't know how to transition to a world where people access shared wealth. It's a very difficult coordination problem because everyone wants as much wealth as possible. So we're starting to use labor as an artificial need signal. I.e. if you need the wealth really badly, jump through these hoops, and you will prove yourself deserving of that wealth. Even the Soviets forced people to make surplus steel because they didn't know how to just give wealth without using labor as a filter. Unfortunately, this means that in the limit 99% of labor will be pretty much useless.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed. — 16 replies — 42.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-07-04 10:51 UTC

OP

Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Now many crypto's like XRP and Chainlink are no longer illegal and the banks can start buying it.

Are you ready for the pump of a lifetime in the coming weeks?

Early replies (first 3)

62444894
I just took 70mg of illicit amphetamine sulfate

62444895
>>62444894
That's very nice to hear, I hope you are enjoying yourself.

62444904
>>62444895
it's nothing like meth (oral ROA meth had no euphoria either), minimal vasoconstriction; none really. not euphoric like adderall either, it feels like super non-diuretic caffeine. this has a 1:1 dextro:racemic ratio; so the pharma equivalent is evekeo.

I thought it was bunk but my heart rate is elevated but no arrhythmia like meth (that shit is poison, it'll keep you up for 3-5 days with no perceptual fatigue at significant cost)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444906
>>62444895
I'm just irritated unless im working or reading something. which is the intended effect, it's just super mechanical caffeine idk how to describe it. it's impossible to "idle" on this shit, I have to be doing something.

62444911
>>62444904
>>62444906
okay well thats nice and all but not really what my thread was about

62444922
>>62444911
hm, do you know how it impacts privacy coins like XMR or even zioncash (ZEC)?

62444929
>>62444922
i do not

62444934
>>62444929
alright, I appreciate it regardless. thoughts on CBDCs?

62444939
>>62444934

I actually dont know much about that also

62444940
>>62444939
you sound like a nice guy no homo

62444948
these 2 got married y'know

62444959
>>62444940
Haha thanks man you too

>>62444948
Nooo we are just friends haha

62444978
>>62444892
> CLARITY ACT
wasn't there some announcement announced for yesterday?

62444991
op they haven't voted on it and aren't close to voting on it. they're hoping to get it done before august 10

62444995
>>62444991
Somone told me clarity act was 4th of july a few weeks ago

62445015
>>62444948
naw, the no homo made marriage impossible
>>62444959
>we are just friends
this
